item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "skeletonized" sounds like my kind of party!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gaia's Avengers Radical Defense Network? Wilderness Encroachment/Extinction Disruption Service!

Ok, ok, I'll stop. In all seriousness, still love this one, glad to see it's still going. =]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see what you did there. I love it. But no. XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…a name that sounds like a snazzy "hippie eco-terrorist-anti-humanity sorcerors…" group

Terrestrial Resistance Earth Emancipation Society?

…without being ridiculous.

Oh. NM…=op

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, wow, been a while since I got a comment here… But yeah, you pretty much nailed the idea I was going for. They knew this was going to do something horrible to the loggers, but they expected to be protected by the source of the power that they used to make 1910, which I've considered going into detail later on down the line with a tale or two, and maybe another skip (which has been sitting in the back of my mind), once I come up with a name that sounds like a snazzy "hippie eco-terrorist-anti-humanity sorcerors" group without being ridiculous.

As far as your second point, I'll agree to disagree purely on the fact that I prefer to keep artistic licence here, and feel that the narrative is slightly more important than dead-set clinical accuracy. If someone has suggestions for a happy medium, I'm all ears.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This twangs a nerve that most Hippy Comeuppance stories on this site don't, namely of how fundamentally fucking horrifying nature itself can actually be. My preferred reading here isn't that the hippies blundered into this unknowing; I like to think that they were aware of exactly what was going to happen, but in sticking to their convictions revealed a stark, deeply unsettling inhumanity in the natural world. The version of Gaia they're protecting has tentacles and insect-parts and mouths where mouths should not be.

Upvoted.

E: And for what it's worth, I'm going to disagree with Photosynthetic's preference for layman terminology here. The clumsiness of the alternate phrasing suggested is, to me, the better choice, the artificiality of the prose in that case actually setting off well against the subject matter. Assholes and opinions, so forth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's the proper term: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Skeletonization

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this (upvoted), but the term skeletonized does not seem to fit the overall tone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Lol glad to hear you liked it. I just wanted to get it as far away as conceivably (sp?) possible from my neck'a the woods here in the Carolinas.

Also, as an open question, I seem to be getting very mixed reviews about the recovery log. Should I maybe fiddle around with that a bit, do y'all think?

Also also, @ Peannlui: So sorry to have missed your comment a couple'a months ago. That part I definitely plan to fix, since a few people have brought it up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This would happen here in Oregon, wouldn't it :-D

+1

Recovery log definitely sold this one. (Not to say the rest was bad, but I'm not sure I would've upvoted.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are some mini hookahs which come to about 10 inches in height (and have their own little boxes to carry them around in), so I don't think there's a need to completely change the object itself, but specify it's dimensions instead?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Looks like you were right xD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll keep your suggestion in mind if the rating nosedives… Its a good idea, but I don't wanna roll the dice if I've already got a decent rating

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would've kinda been more amenable to a bong that was described but never actually called a bong (thus delayed revelation "…wait… this is a… OHH"), because a hookah seems a bit awkward, but I still thought this was great. I'm surprised not to have read this effect before, actually. It's a nice bit of reciprocity horror.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, previous people posted on how vague I had left the activation conditions, so I tightened them up by making it essentially only activate in the conditions it was intended for, smoking mu'assel or hashish. The way I had it in my head, they wouldn't be likely to try to fill it with anything else like, say, paper, as your suggestion would imply… And not all hookahs are giant contraptions, I've seen some that are relatively small and easy to carry around, and I tried to indicate that in the description. I do like the incense burner idea, though. If I get more people indicating the difficulty/awkwardness of the hookah, I may try that, or the shamanic pipe idea. I'm just sketchy on changing it to something outright spiritual/mystic, as I mentioned before…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What about using a incense brazier instead of a hookah? Much smaller and more portable, still used by hippies. Also could imply a date at least as old as a hookah.

Like others have said, the necessity of tobacco and/or hashish seems oddly specific to me. I would think that burning any plant-based material would be sufficient to incite the fog.

The recovery is a low point for me. Somehow trite.

Still, the core idea of a flesh-eating fog that protects plants through freaky medicine is interesting, visual, and imaginative. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That smoothed the rough down nicely for me. Good stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for the acquisition log, though the effects are pretty original by themselves. I was kind of happy to see no Fifthist links.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nicely done. Damn hippies!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one. Makes me think of Alice in Wonderland and just how horrifying that caterpillar could really have been.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I attempted to address all stated issues here (containment redaction, activation conditions, size of the hookah being weird), except for the "patch" bit, which I felt was adequately explained by Photosynthetic: it would be too difficult and awkward to state it in any other way, and it gets the idea across pretty clearly

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Weird and simple. I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not voting on this because I had too much of a hand in its construction, but it would have my +1 if I could. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That bit is my fault, so I'll try to explain. I used the layman's term because the literal terminology would be something to the effect of "The fog will then use the dissolved animal tissue to repair any damaged plants inside its radius, replacing destroyed tissues and organs with healthy equivalents derived from that animal tissue", which was thoroughly awkward. Layman's-terms, plus scare-quotes to indicate that it's not technical phrasing, seemed a better option.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Site ██.

Redaction in the containment procedures. In this case, though, it's not that bad.

when filled and lit properly,

With? Does it require marijuana, or can you use tobacco?

Besides that, that's it. Very well done, and very cool. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yay for hookah SCPs!

The SCP evocates some pretty nasty pictures and the recovery log is pretty good.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
replacing destroyed tissues and organs with healthy "patches".

I don't think that this is the correct way to use citation: who is being quoted here? Why can't you just describe the phenemenon instead of loosely inserting laymen's terms?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very good.
Very nice biologic effect, and a good recovery log story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Hippie comeuppance" made me chuckle… And yeah, I didnt want to go into some giant explanation/overly squicky description, because I felt that would detract from the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Woah, man. This is like, some heavy shit. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this. The idea of well meaning but misguided hippies bringing out an SCP which they've been told will, like, help the trees, man, but end up in over their heads is one I really like. Basically any story of hippy comeuppance.

Also, the horror of trees being made out of reclaimed human tissue is nice and horrifying, even without a ton of explanation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short, sweet, and a little spooky. +1 for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mostly chose hookah because saying it was a bong made me (and others) giggle, and a shaman's pipe felt toosupernatural… But I see your point. A hookah would be a bit unwieldy in this situation. I'll see what I can come up with in the next day or so.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neat treatment of the Gaias Vengeance concept. The use of a hookah as the vector comes off a little stiff to me, I think a bong might work better or maybe some kind of shamanesque pipe? Lugging around/using a hookah under the conditions described would be awkward as hell, is the main thing that strikes me as a bit off, plus a hookah is a pretty heavily engineered piece of hardware, I feel like it might flow better using something with more of a close-to-nature vibe. Dunno. I know it doesn't bug me enough to not upvote though, good job.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah. Whoops XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You had SCP 1910 instead of SCP-1910.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the title thing. Thought I had gotten it. What did I mess up there, so I know to look out for that on the chance I end up posting something else in the future?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you can figure out what's off, be sure to let me know, so I can try to fix it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed the title. Also found it to be good. Not great, but good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm. There's something odd about this one, but I can't put my finger on what. I certainly don't dislike it, but I'm not particularly impressed either. No vote, but well done for a first entry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it; however, the tag corrosive, I really don't think fits.

item.title: My first time
item.content:encoded: Heya everyone, I've received a TON of help on this, so I would like to thank, in no particular order, Djoric for helping me come up with the idea and being a good mentor overall through the process, Snakeoil Sage for helping me ground it in a specific object and time period, and Photosynthetic for helping me nail the biology bits (as well as writing style) down. Also, the dozen or so people in chat that gave me feedback. Here goes nothing~
